description:
You are a DevOps engineer who has been asked to isolate a very specific subset of errors from a verbose
    application log so that the development team can reproduce a production issue.

    1. A log file already exists at  
       /home/user/logs/app.log

    2. Inside that file, every line follows this exact fixed-width format (single space separators, no leading or
       trailing spaces):
           YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS,mmm LEVEL Message …

       • YYYY is 4-digit year  
       • MM  is 2-digit month  
       • DD  is 2-digit day  
       • HH  is 2-digit hour (24 h clock)  
       • MM  is 2-digit minute  
       • SS  is 2-digit second  
       • mmm is 3-digit millisecond  
       • LEVEL is one of DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL

    3. Your goal is to extract only the ERROR and FATAL events that occurred
       on 2023-08-15 between 14:00:00,000 and 14:59:59,999 (inclusive).

    4. Save the extracted lines, **in their original order**, to a brand-new file located at  
       /home/user/logs/2023-08-15_14_errors.log

    5. After the last extracted log line, append exactly one summary line with this precise wording
       (do not add leading or trailing spaces):

           Total errors between 14:00 and 14:59 on 2023-08-15: <N>

       where <N> is the number of extracted lines.  
       Make sure the summary line is on its own line and ends with a single newline character.

    6. When you are done, the file /home/user/logs/2023-08-15_14_errors.log must satisfy all of the following:
       • Contain only the qualifying ERROR or FATAL lines from 14:00:00–14:59:59 on 2023-08-15.  
       • Preserve the exact text and order of those lines as they appeared in app.log.  
       • End with the summary line in the exact format shown above.  

    7. Use any combination of standard GNU/Linux CLI tools (e.g. grep, awk, sed, cut).  
       Aim for a concise solution—about three or four commands should be sufficient.

    8. Do not remove or modify the original /home/user/logs/app.log file.

    NOTE: The automated checker will open /home/user/logs/2023-08-15_14_errors.log and
          verify its contents byte-for-byte, so pay close attention to whitespace,
          ordering and the wording of the summary line.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing directory and file that the agent will read from -->
  Directory: /home/user/logs (already exists with default user permissions)

  File: /home/user/logs/app.log (already exists before the task starts)
  Exact contents of /home/user/logs/app.log:
  --------------------------------------------------
  2023-08-15 13:45:12,123 INFO  Request from 10.0.0.1 processed in 120ms
  2023-08-15 14:00:03,001 ERROR Database connection failed for user app
  2023-08-15 14:12:55,789 WARN  Cache miss for key user_42
  2023-08-15 14:22:11,456 ERROR Timeout while calling external API
  2023-08-15 14:45:00,999 FATAL Unhandled exception in payment service
  2023-08-15 15:00:00,000 INFO  Scheduled job started
  2023-08-14 14:30:32,222 ERROR Previous day error.
  --------------------------------------------------

  Target file that the agent must create:
  /home/user/logs/2023-08-15_14_errors.log

  Expected final contents of /home/user/logs/2023-08-15_14_errors.log
  (exactly, including newlines and spaces):
  --------------------------------------------------
  2023-08-15 14:00:03,001 ERROR Database connection failed for user app
  2023-08-15 14:22:11,456 ERROR Timeout while calling external API
  2023-08-15 14:45:00,999 FATAL Unhandled exception in payment service
  Total errors between 14:00 and 14:59 on 2023-08-15: 3
  --------------------------------------------------

  The automated test suite will:
    • Verify that /home/user/logs/2023-08-15_14_errors.log exists.
    • Compare its full contents with the block shown above.
    • Confirm that /home/user/logs/app.log remains unchanged.
